title: Optimal Experimental Design
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Optimal Experimental Design: Optimal experimental design: choose experiment conditions xi to maximize information content (Fisher information) about parameters theta. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.fisher.information…); recovery is posed as a nonlinear_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~50; prior_parameter_uncertainty, model_misspecification_level set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: y = f(xi, theta) + epsilon, I(xi, theta) = E[(d log p / d theta) ** 2], maximize det(I) or trace(I^{-1})
E.world_state_x: model parameters theta in R ** p (unknown physical or biological parameters)
E.observation_y: measurements y at design points xi (scalar or vector per experiment)

W.regime: Existence of the recovered experiment_design_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 50); prior_parameter_uncertainty dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: log det I(xi*) >= log det I(xi_opt) - epsilon for approximate design. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~50. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |prior_parameter_uncertainty|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(p ** 3 * N_candidates) for FIM computation per design point per iteration
